questions.title: Count Vowels
questions.prompt: Count how many vowels appear in a given string. Treat uppercase and lowercase vowels the same.
hints: Make every letter lowercase first so A and a are treated the same.
Keep the vowels in a small lookup group: a, e, i, o, u.
Walk through the string one character at a time and add 1 only when the character is a vowel.

questions.title: Reverse Words
questions.prompt: Given a sentence, return the words in reverse order while keeping each word's letters unchanged.
hints: Split the sentence into words.
Reverse the list of words.
Join the reversed words with single spaces.

questions.title: Maximum Score
questions.prompt: Return the largest integer in a list of assignment scores. If the list is empty, return null or the language equivalent.
hints: Handle the empty list first.
Track the best value seen so far.
Compare each score against the current best.

questions.title: Is Palindrome
questions.prompt: Return true if a string reads the same forward and backward after ignoring spaces and case.
constraints: Input may contain spaces and letters.
hints: Normalize case and remove spaces.
Compare characters from both ends.
Stop early if a mismatch appears.

questions.title: Sum Even Numbers
questions.prompt: Given a list of integers, return the sum of only the even numbers.
hints: Start the sum at zero.
Check divisibility by 2.
Add only values that pass the even check.

questions.title: First Repeated Character
questions.prompt: Return the first character that appears more than once while scanning left to right. Return null if none repeat.
hints: Keep a set of seen characters.
Scan left to right.
The first character already in the set is the answer.

questions.title: Grade Bucket
questions.prompt: Convert a numeric score into A, B, C, D, or F using standard 90/80/70/60 cutoffs.
hints: Check highest cutoffs first.
Use if/else branches.
Return once the correct bucket is found.

questions.title: Remove Duplicates Keep Order
questions.prompt: Return a new list with duplicate values removed while preserving the first occurrence order.
hints: Use a set to remember values already copied.
Keep a result list.
Append only when the value has not been seen.

questions.title: Count Words
questions.prompt: Count how many words are in a sentence. Multiple spaces should not create empty words.
hints: Trim or split on whitespace.
Ignore empty pieces.
Return the number of real words.

questions.title: Smallest Positive
questions.prompt: Return the smallest positive integer in a list. Return null if there is no positive integer.
hints: Track a best positive value.
Ignore zero and negative values.
Update the best when a smaller positive appears.

questions.title: Running Total
questions.prompt: Return a list where each position contains the sum of all values up to that position.
hints: Keep a running sum.
Add each number to the running sum.
Append the running sum to the result.

questions.title: Valid Course Code Shape
questions.prompt: Return true if a course code looks like four uppercase letters, optional space, then three digits, such as COSC 220 or MATH241.
constraints: Do not validate against the catalog, only the shape.
hints: Ignore one space if it appears between the letters and numbers.
After removing that space, the code should have exactly 7 characters.
Check that the first 4 characters are uppercase letters and the last 3 are digits.

questions.title: Find Index
questions.prompt: Return the index of the first occurrence of a target value in a list, or -1 if it is absent.
hints: Loop over indexes, not only values.
Compare each value with the target.
Return immediately when found.

questions.title: Merge Names
questions.prompt: Given two lists of names, return one list containing all names from the first list followed by all names from the second.
hints: Create a result list.
Add every item from the first list.
Then add every item from the second list.

questions.title: Temperature Above Threshold
questions.prompt: Count how many temperature readings are strictly greater than a threshold.
hints: Initialize a counter.
Compare each reading with the threshold.
Increment only for readings above it.

questions.title: Last Digit
questions.prompt: Given a non-negative integer n, return only its last digit as an integer. For example, 384 should return 4.
hints: The last digit is the remainder after dividing by 10.
Use n % 10 to get that remainder.
Check n = 0 too; its last digit should still be 0.

questions.title: Truthy Attendance
questions.prompt: Given a list of booleans for attendance, count how many values are true.
hints: Start a counter at zero.
Check each value directly.
Increment for true values.

questions.title: Initials
questions.prompt: Given a full name, return the uppercase initials of each word.
hints: Split into words.
Take the first character of each word.
Uppercase the collected initials.

questions.title: Clamp Score
questions.prompt: Clamp a score so values below 0 become 0 and values above 100 become 100.
hints: Check the lower bound.
Check the upper bound.
Return the original score when it is inside range.

questions.title: Every Other Item
questions.prompt: Return a new list containing every other item from the input, starting at index 0.
hints: Loop indexes by 2.
Start at index 0.
Append the selected values to a result list.

questions.title: Count Digits
questions.prompt: Return how many digits are in a non-negative integer. For 0, return 1.
hints: Start with the special case: 0 has one digit.
For any other number, remove the last digit by dividing by 10 and keeping the whole-number part.
Count each removal until the number becomes 0.

questions.title: Matrix Column Sums
questions.prompt: Given a rectangular matrix of integers, return a list with the sum of each column.
constraints: All non-empty rows have the same length.
hints: If the matrix has no rows, return an empty list right away.
Use the first row to know how many column totals you need.
For each row, add row[0] to column total 0, row[1] to column total 1, and so on.

questions.title: Can Vote
questions.prompt: Return true if an age is at least 18, otherwise return false.
hints: The cutoff is inclusive, so age 18 should return true.
Compare the age with 18 using greater-than-or-equal.
Return the result of that comparison instead of writing extra branches.

questions.title: Unique Count
questions.prompt: Given a list of integers, return how many different values appear at least once. Repeated values should only count one time.
hints: Use a set because it stores one copy of each value.
Add each number from the list into the set.
The answer is the number of items left in the set.
